stemHtml:
A student pilot carried out two identical cross-country flights at 2 000 ft, one in the afternoon and the other just after sunrise. On returning from the early morning cross-country flight the student asked why the flight was so much smoother and easier to fly.

How can this observation be explained?

options.a: The increased stability and reduced convection likely decreased the upper limit of the turbulent friction layer to below 2 000 ft on the early morning cross-country flight.
options.b: The low-level winds at night, and in the early morning, are usually much stronger and more laminar compared to those in the afternoon.
options.c: The strong nocturnal inversion present in the early morning most likely stopped turbulence and wind shear.
options.d: In the early morning, the changes in stability and the lighter winds lift the friction layer to well above 2 000 ft.
